title: tpm — faaas_kiwi_project_pptx — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=faaas_kiwi_project_pptx. Refine before promoting.

report_title.description: Provide the slide title in exec-safe language. Use the program name from the Confluence page and append 'Executive Update'.

reporting_date.description: Set the 'as of' date for this update. If the Confluence page explicitly states a last-updated/as-of date, use it; otherwise use today's date at generation time and label it 'As of <date>'.

project_purpose.description: Extract the one-sentence goal/purpose of the FAaaS Kiwi project from the Confluence page introduction/goal statement. Keep it exec-ready and avoid internal-only acronyms unless expanded.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single RAG status (Green/Amber/Red) plus a 1-sentence justification by summarising the Confluence page signals: progress metrics (scan coverage, completion counts), explicit leadership decisions needed, and stated capacity constraints. Format: '<RAG> — <justification>'.

orm_status.description: Derive an ORM-style status label from the Confluence page risk/ask signals. If explicit ORM wording is absent, infer a concise status such as 'On track', 'At risk', or 'Off track' based on blockers/decisions/capacity constraints described on the page; do not fabricate numeric ORM scores.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health ('On track'/'At risk'/'Off track') by combining any dated events (e.g., proposal review dates, meeting dates) with evidence of missing decisions/capacity constraints. If no milestones/baseline exist, state 'At risk — schedule baseline not stated; progress depends on <key dependency>'.

metrics_snapshot.description: Extract 3–6 exec-friendly numeric metrics from the Confluence page (e.g., scan coverage %, repos scanned/total, additional repos submitted, Ksplice completion counts). Return as short strings like 'Security scan coverage: 97% (259/266 repos)'.

workstreams_summary.description: Summarise the four workstreams listed on the Confluence page (#1 security scanning, #2 fleet patching, #3 DB patching, #4 OS patching). For each, provide a single concise status sentence derived by summarising the corresponding workstream section text; do not list every sub-detail.

blocking_issues.description: Derive blockers by aggregating statements on the Confluence page that indicate a dependency, decision needed, capacity constraint, or required ER/action that is preventing progress (e.g., leadership clarity on monthly DB patching; non-Ksplice capacity slots; ERs needed). For each blocker, output an object with: description, owner (team/person if stated), target_resolution (date if stated else 'TBD'), critical_path (true if it gates monthly patching/scanning completion). If none, return [].

next_steps.description: Derive next steps by combining explicit action items and implied immediate actions from the Confluence page (e.g., finalize fleet patching proposal, file ERs, decide DB patching requirement/scope). Each item must be time-bound using a stated date from the page; if no date is stated, use 'this sprint' as the time bound. Keep each item to one line.

exec_asks.description: Derive executive asks by aggregating explicit 'leadership ask' language and decision requests from the Confluence page (e.g., approve monthly fleet patching approach; clarify whether monthly DB patching is required; confirm patching scope). Each ask must be phrased as a decision or approval request and include why it matters in 6–12 words.

decisions_needed.description: Extract and consolidate decision points from the Confluence page (e.g., whether DB patching every month is required; what patching scope types apply; ownership options under review). Return as short strings; avoid duplicates with exec_asks by keeping this list purely as decision statements.

key_links.description: Extract the most useful 4–8 links from the Confluence page for executives (PMO ticket, Jira dashboard, Jira filter, security dashboard, repo list, supporting materials/xls). Return as strings formatted 'Label — URL'.

do_not_invoke_if_phrases.description: Phrases that indicate the request is not about the FAaaS Kiwi project; if any are present in the user request/context, do not invoke this skill and ask for clarification. Must include: 'mango project', 'not kiwi', 'different project'.

capacity_constraints.description: Extract 1–4 explicit capacity constraints from the Confluence page (e.g., non-Ksplice patching slots available vs needed). Format each as a short string like 'Non-Ksplice patching capacity: 2 slots/month available vs 5 needed (3 days each)'. If none stated, return [].

dependencies_external_teams.description: List key external teams/partners mentioned as dependencies or collaborators (e.g., SaaS Security POCs, Erna’s team). Return 1–6 short strings; include why they matter in 4–10 words when stated/implied.
